body.title: deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud hangs on b5-frobenius 40% of the time — and frontier has no measured holder
body.text:
MEASURED 2026-07-09. Controlled experiment: same query, same temperature 0, same 1800s timeout; ONE variable, max_tokens. 5 samples at 4096 and 5 at 32768, plus an earlier 2-call probe.

  mt=4096   43, HANG, 43, HANG, 43        (+probe 43)   -> 4/4 correct on valid samples
  mt=32768  43, HANG, 43, HANG, 37        (+probe 37)   -> 2/4 correct on valid samples

FINDING 1 — max_tokens does NOT determine the answer. Both wrong answers fell at 32768 and none at 4096, which is suggestive, but 4/4 vs 2/4 is nowhere near significant (Fisher p ~ 0.43). The model is UNSTABLE on this query at temperature 0. An earlier single sample at each budget (43 vs 37) nearly became a claim that the budget was semantic; two samples per cell would still not have caught it. One sample is a coin.

FINDING 2 — QUERY-SPECIFIC NON-TERMINATION. 6 hangs across 15 calls on b5-frobenius (40%). ZERO hangs across 30 calls on the other 15 corpus queries. Every hang consumed the full timeout (600s in the sweep, 1800s here) on a call the model otherwise completes in 213-253s with 1500-2400 output tokens. This is not provider flakiness spread across traffic; it is one prompt.

FINDING 3 — A TIMEOUT DESTROYS ITS OWN EVIDENCE. A hung call returns nothing: no finish_reason, no text, raw=null. So the one place a degenerate decoding loop could hide is exactly the place we cannot look. Note degeneracy-not-observed-20260709 measured 226 reasoning traces and found zero degenerate cases — but every one of those was a TRUNCATION (a call that returned). The hang is a different state and remains unexplained.

CONSEQUENCE FOR THE ROUTER — the `frontier` rung has no measured holder.
  - deepseek-r1:14b  ($0, local): b5 4/4 clean, 0 truncations, 0 errors. Fails b4-boxes.
  - deepseek-r1:32b  ($0, local): b5 3/4 (fails b5-frobenius at 4096, 8192 AND 32768, clean stop).
  - deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud    : b5 3/4 with 1 ERR — the b5-frobenius cell HAS NO VERDICT.
  - llama3.2:3b      ($0, local): b5 0/4.

The guard (tests/inference/test_capability_evidence.py) therefore REFUSES the 671b's frontier claim on two independent grounds: a model that errored at the ceiling has holes in its measurement, and a TOP claim requires a clean pass of the top band. Both assertions are correct and both are red.

So the situation is: the `frontier` bucket was ADDED (T-inference-cost-first-sort-strands-cloud-fleet) so escalation could leave the local box, on the assumption that the cloud model was more capable. Measured, on this corpus, that assumption is unsupported. The 671b is not measurably better than a $0 local 14b on the only query that has ever separated two models — and on that query it cannot reliably even RETURN.

THIS IS A DESIGN DECISION FOR AKIEN, NOT A CODE FIX. The options, none taken:
  (a) Drop the 671b to `declared` and accept an EMPTY top bucket. Honest, and escalation's last hop then always halts — test_the_top_bucket_is_not_empty goes red, correctly.
  (b) Remove the `frontier` bucket entirely. Reverts to 3 buckets and re-opens the original defect: escalation can never leave the local box. But we now have no evidence that leaving it helps.
  (c) Rebuild the corpus so the top band DISCRIMINATES (b5 is fame-contaminated: Chicken McNugget, knights-and-knaves, CRT, stars-and-bars — a 14b clears it; b4-boxes leaks its answer token 'both' twice in the prompt). Then re-measure. This is the only option that produces new information rather than re-labelling old.
  (d) Investigate the hang first — a top rung that returns nothing 40% of the time is unusable regardless of its capability.

CONDITIONS: 2026-07-09; corpus escalation_corpus (16 queries); temperature 0; ollama_cloud subscription; timeouts 600s (sweep) and 1800s (stability). Every number true only for these conditions.

RELATED: capability-ceiling-sweep note (the matrix), degeneracy-not-observed-20260709, T-capability-measured-at-a-budget-ceiling, T-truncation-vs-degeneracy-third-outcome, memory feedback_capability_is_not_a_ladder, feedback_eval_queries_must_not_leak.
